config.section.network_shares.label: Local network shares
config.section.network_shares.desc: Manage SMB and NFS shares provided by this host.
config.network_shares.loading: Reading local SMB and NFS capabilities…
config.network_shares.client_placeholder: IP address or CIDR, for example 192.168.1.0/24
config.network_shares.no_shares: No visible shares inside the allowed roots.
config.network_shares.name: Name
config.network_shares.delete_message: Remove the share “{name}”? The directory and all files remain untouched.
config.network_shares.save_first: Save the configuration before using host actions.
config.network_shares.status_ready: At least one local sharing protocol is ready.
config.network_shares.deleted: Share definition removed. Files were not deleted.
help.network_shares.enabled: Exposes only installed and configured local SMB/NFS services. AuraGo never installs packages or starts services.
help.network_shares.readonly: Overrides all create, change, and remove permissions.
help.network_shares.allow_create: Allows creating new AuraGo-owned share definitions.
help.network_shares.allow_update: Allows changing access and descriptions of AuraGo-owned shares.
help.network_shares.allow_delete: Removes only AuraGo-owned share definitions, never directories or files.
help.network_shares.roots: Absolute roots only. Share directories must already exist and remain inside these roots after resolving links.
help.network_shares.smb_enabled: Uses Samba registry shares on Linux or the installed SMBShare module on Windows.
help.network_shares.allow_guest: Guest access must be explicitly enabled and is never offered on Windows.
help.network_shares.principals: Existing operating-system users or groups only. AuraGo does not manage accounts or passwords.
help.network_shares.nfs_enabled: Uses AuraGo-owned exports.d files on Linux or the installed NFS module on Windows.
help.network_shares.clients: Explicit IP addresses or CIDRs only. Wildcards and no_root_squash are not allowed.
security.hint.network_shares_write_enabl: AuraGo may change or remove local SMB/NFS share definitions. Keep roots, principals, and clients narrow; removing a share never deletes its files.
